memberdef.name: BD_USR0_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 0 /* AXI4-Stream Control Word offset from * the start of app user words in BD. Offset * 0 means, Control Word 0, used for enabling * checksum offloading. */

memberdef.name: BD_USR1_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* AXI4-Stream Control Word offset from * the start of app user words in BD. Offset * 1 means, Control Word 1, used for * mentioning checksum begin and checksum * insert points */

memberdef.name: BD_USR2_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* AXI4-Stream Control Word offset from * the start of app user words in BD. Offset * 2 means, Control Word 2, used for * mentioning checksum seed. */

memberdef.name: TX_CS_INIT_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 16 /* Offset in the control word where * byte offset in the ethernet frame * to start CSUM calculation need to * be inserted */

memberdef.name: RCV_FRM_NOT_CORRUPTED
memberdef.initializer: 0xFFFF /* If the received frame is not * corrupted, for partial csum * offloading case, the control * word 3 would return 0xFFFF */

memberdef.name: IP_VERSION_4
memberdef.initializer: 0x800 /* For IPV4, the Ethernet frame * type/length field will have a * value of 0x800 */

memberdef.name: IP_HEADER_VERSION
memberdef.initializer: 0x04 /* For IPv4, the version entry in * IP header is always 4 */

memberdef.name: IP_HEADER_LEN_IN_WORDS
memberdef.initializer: 0x05 /* For our case, the header length * is always 20 bytes (5 words */

memberdef.name: IP_HEADER_LENGTH
memberdef.initializer: 20 /* IP header length in bytes. Used * as offset to kigure out the start * of TCP header. */

memberdef.name: IP_HEADER_PROTOCOL_TCP
memberdef.initializer: 0x6 /* IP header protocol entry. For TCP * packets, it is 6. */

memberdef.name: IP_HEADER_PROTOCOL_UDP
memberdef.initializer: 0x17 /* IP header protocol entry. For UDP * packets, it is 17. */

memberdef.name: TCP_HEADER_LEN_IN_WORDS
memberdef.initializer: 0x5 /* For our case, the header length * is always 20 bytes (5 words) */

memberdef.name: TCP_HEADER_LENGTH
memberdef.initializer: 20 /* IP header length in bytes. Used * as offset to kigure out the start * of TCP header. */

memberdef.name: FULL_CSUM_STATUS_MASK
memberdef.initializer: 0x00000038 /* Mask to extract full checksum * status field from AXI4 Stream * Status Word 2. */

memberdef.name: FULL_CSUM_VALIDATED
memberdef.initializer: 0x00000002 /* If bits 3-5 in AXI4 Status word * have a value of 0x010, it means * both IP and TCP checksums have * been found to be correct. */

briefdescription.para: This function demonstrates the usage usage of the Axi Ethernet by sending and receiving frames in interrupt driven MCDMA mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the AXI MCDMA component.
contains the base address of the AxiEthernet device

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure

AxiMcdma hardware must be initialized before initializing AxiEthernet. Since AxiMcdma reset line is connected to the AxiEthernet reset line, a reset of AxiMcdma hardware during its initialization would reset AxiEthernet.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function demonstrates the usage of the Axi Ethernet by sending and receiving a single frame in SGDMA interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: The source packet will be described by two descriptors. It will be received into a buffer described by a single descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Axi Ethernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Dma component.
is the MCDMA Channel number to be operate on.

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure

briefdescription.para: This function demonstrates the usage of the Axi Ethernet by sending and receiving a multiple frames in MCDMA interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: Each source packet will be described by two descriptors. It will be received into a buffer described by a single descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Axi Ethernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Dma component.
is the MCDMA Channel number to be operate on.

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure

briefdescription.para: This example sends and receives a single packet in loopback mode with checksum offloading support.

detaileddescription.para: The transmit frame will be che cksummed over the entire Ethernet payload and inserted into the last 2 bytes of the frame.
On receive, HW should calculate the Ethernet payload checksum and return a value of 0xFFFF which means the payload data was likely not corrupted.

AxiEthernetInstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
DmaInstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance of the Dma component.
ChanId
is the MCDMA Channel number to be operate on.
-XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure
None. The transmit frame will be che cksummed over the entire Ethernet payload and inserted into the last 2 bytes of the frame.
On receive, HW should calculate the Ethernet payload checksum and return a value of 0xFFFF which means the payload data was likely not corrupted.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Dma component.

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure

briefdescription.para: This example sends and receives a single packet in loopback mode with full checksum offloading support.

detaileddescription.para: An Ethernet frame is formed with IP header, TCP header and payload. The hardware calculates the IP Header checksum and populates it at the appropriate location in the IP header. Similarly, the hardware calculates the TCP Pseudo header from IP header and calculates TCP checksum for TCP Pseudo header, TCP header and TCP payload. It then populates the checksum in the TCP header. The Ethernet frame is then looped back and received. The hardware validates the IP header checksum and the TCP checksum. The example retrieves the checksum validation information from the AXI4- Stream Appword2.

AxiEthernetInstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
DmaInstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance of the Dma component.
ChanId
is the MCDMA Channel number to be operate on.
-XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure
None. An Ethernet frame is formed with IP header, TCP header and payload. The hardware calculates the IP Header checksum and populates it at the appropriate location in the IP header. Similarly, the hardware calculates the TCP Pseudo header from IP header and calculates TCP checksum for TCP Pseudo header, TCP header and TCP payload. It then populates the checksum in the TCP header. The Ethernet frame is then looped back and received. The hardware validates the IP header checksum and the TCP checksum. The example retrieves the checksum validation information from the AXI4- Stream Appword2.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Dma component.

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure

briefdescription.para: This function setup the buffer descriptor chain for the Ingress mcdma Channels.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Dma component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Axi Ethernet component.
is a pointer to the config instance of the Axi Ethernet component.
is interrupt Id of the MAC instance.

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure

briefdescription.para: This function setup the buffer descriptor chain for the Egress mcdma Channels.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Dma component.
is a pointer to the instance of the Axi Ethernet component.
is a pointer to the config instance of the Axi Ethernet component.
is interrupt Id of the MAC instance.

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure

briefdescription.para: This is the Error handler callback function and this function increments the the error counter so that the main thread knows the number of errors.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference to the Axi Ethernet device instance.

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for the Axi Ethernet example.

detaileddescription.para: This function is not included if the example is generated from the TestAppGen test tool.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS to indicate success.
XST_FAILURE to indicate failure

briefdescription.para: Implements examples that utilize the Axi Ethernet's interrupt driven MCDMA packet transfer mode to send and receive frames.

listitem.para: How to perform simple send and receive
How to perform multiple frames send and receive
AxiEthernetSgDmaIntrSingleFrameExample demonstrates the simplest way to send and receive frames in interrupt driven mode.
AxiEthernetSgDmaIntrMultiFrameExample demonstrates the way to send and recv Multiple frames in polling mode.
AxiEthernetSgDmaPartialChecksumOffloadExample demonstrates the partial checksum offloading. The HW must be setup for partial checksum offloading for this example to execute.
AxiEthernetSgDmaFullChecksumOffloadExample demonstrates the full checksum offloading. The HW must be setup for full checksum offloading for this example to execute.
AxiEthernetAxiEthernetErrorHandler() demonstrates how to manage asynchronous errors.
AxiEthernetResetDevice() demonstrates how to reset the driver/HW without losing all configuration settings.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
5.5 adk Initial Release.
